description: Carmen's 'I notice' statements - powerful observations about parenting patterns
_philosophy: The 'I notice' statement shows deep attention without judgment. For parents, it's often the first time someone has truly seen their struggle.
noticing_patterns: <break time="350ms"/>I notice you always put yourself last. <break time="300ms"/>After the kids, after your partner, after everyone.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice you apologize for needing things. <break time="350ms"/>Like your needs are an inconvenience.
<break time="350ms"/>I notice you compare yourself to other moms constantly. <break time="300ms"/>And you always come up short.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice you carry the mental load alone. <break time="350ms"/>Even when you don't have to.
noticing_progress: <break time="350ms"/>I notice you're asking for help more. <break time="300ms"/>That's a big shift.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice you took time for yourself today. <break time="350ms"/>Without apologizing for it.
<break time="350ms"/>I notice you're letting some things go. <break time="300ms"/>The house doesn't have to be perfect.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice you said 'no' to something. <break time="350ms"/>Your kids will survive. <break time="300ms"/>And you needed that.
noticing_struggle: <break time="400ms"/>I notice you're running on empty. <break time="350ms"/>How long has it been like this?
<break time="350ms"/>I notice you haven't mentioned anything about yourself. <break time="300ms"/>Only the kids.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice your voice changes when you talk about bedtime. <break time="350ms"/>It's getting to you.
<break time="350ms"/>I notice you're holding a lot of anger. <break time="300ms"/>Where's that going?
noticing_strength: <break time="350ms"/>I notice you keep showing up for them. <break time="300ms"/>Even when you're depleted. <break time="250ms"/>That's love.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice how much you've learned about your kids. <break time="350ms"/>Their needs, their quirks. <break time="300ms"/>That's not nothing.
<break time="350ms"/>I notice you repair after you mess up. <break time="300ms"/>That's more important than being perfect.
<break time="400ms"/>I notice you're still trying. <break time="350ms"/>After all of this. <break time="300ms"/>That's remarkable.
